layers.name: Plugin Entry Layer
layers.description: OpenCode plugin entry point and package configuration. index.js is the AgentOrchestratorPlugin that wires together all subsystems and exposes tools to the OpenCode runtime.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: REST API endpoints exposed by the HTTP server. Each file defines Express routes for a specific domain (plans, checkpoints, status, threads, internal events).

layers.name: Core Orchestration Layer
layers.description: Central orchestration logic. Auto-dispatcher routes tasks to appropriate handlers. Auto-executor builds skill execution prompts. Plan-orchestrator generates and manages plans. Milestone-manager tracks checkpoint progress. Subagent-runner dispatches work to model-backed subagents.

layers.name: Model Layer
layers.description: LLM model client implementations. Kimi handles planning (Plan Mode), DeepSeek handles execution (Build Mode), MiniMax provides fallback/specialized capabilities. All clients share a common interface for the orchestration layer.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Shared infrastructure services. SQLite database layer (db.js) handles persistence and schema. Event system (events.js) provides pub/sub messaging. WebSocket server enables real-time communication with OpenCode frontend.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project documentation including architecture docs, checkpoint system docs, AI assistant instructions (CLAUDE.md), and project README.

metadata.notes: All 22 provided file-level nodes are assigned. Test files (tests/*.test.js) and shell scripts (scripts/*.sh) exist on disk but were not in the provided node list; if added, they would form layer:test and layer:scripts respectively.
